title: China's Coal Import Prospects For This Year Remain Bleak
section_path: China's Coal Import Prospects For This Year Remain Bleak
section_path_text: China's Coal Import Prospects For This Year Remain Bleak
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed for clients in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly China Report, domestic coal production totaled 389.3 million tons in April. This is down month-on-month by 51.3 million tons (-12%), is up year-on-year by 17.6 million tons (5%), and marks an eleventh straight month where China's coal production has grown on a year-on-year basis. Image reference: Chart 1.jpg | assets/2025-06-02_chinas-coal-import-prospects-for-this-year-remain-bleak_img_chart1_ef912c9d0e6e.jpg
Coal-derived electricity generation totaled only 449.1 billion kilowatt hours.
